caption:
The most successful people I know never chase money.

They chase being useful.

Money is a byproduct of value creation.

caption:

caption:
The fastest way to lose respect as a leader: sugarcoat hard truths. Withholding clarity to spare feelings erodes trust.

I shared this memo with my team to encourage them to have the hard conversations they were putting off. 

Every Monday, I share the internal memos I send to my team via my newsletter. If you want them sent to you, comment “ACQ” to sign up.

latestComments.text: Going through this this week, great post

alt: Photo by Leila Hormozi on April 06, 2026. May be an image of magazine, poster and text that says 'ACQUISITION.COM [Internal Memo] From the desk of Leila Hormozi, Executive Chairwoman Early on at Gym Launch, we brought on new Operations Manager. She was not blowing things up like had hoped (and prayed for lol) she was just... okay. Consistently okay. And knew it, but hoped it would turn around sooner than later. But worse than that .... other people on the team knew it too. They were coming to me with feedback about this person's questionable performance. caption:
Newest addition to the office ✨

I wanted this message to be the first thing anyone sees when they enter my office.

Why?

Because it’s what most people need to hear regardless of where they’re at.

Whether you wake up feeling like you can conquer the world, or you feel like it’s crumbling around you.

Stick to the f***king plan

That’s the only way you keep growing.

That’s the only way you create the future you want.

- Leila

caption:
When you choose to improve your life, you will lose friends. People won’t understand why you’re waking up early, going out less, and being “so disciplined”. 

That’s okay.
If you want to be great, be ready to be misunderstood.
firstComment:
I used to think losing friends meant something was wrong with me. But as I grew, I realized I was simply outgrowing people who were comfortable with my old patterns. Growth demands a new environment, and not everyone can breathe at that altitude.
What’s one way your growth changed the people you keep around you?

caption: How to build a culture where employees feel comfortable sharing hard truths: reward them for bringing you bad news.
firstComment: This is such a new perspective; most people say they want honesty, until it challenges them.

latestComments.text: This is such a new perspective; most people say they want honesty, until it challenges them.
Absolutely, what you reward as a leader gets observed and repeated.
I would pay for you to be my boss 🫶🏻 like wow, you need to clone yourself 😂
The moment people learn bad news gets you fired up at them, they stop bringing it. Then problems hide until they explode. The whole point is making it safe to surface problems early.

caption: Beating yourself up only steals energy you could use toward building yourself better.

caption: Your business is only as strong as the person leading it.

caption:
If you’re applying for jobs, make sure your values align with the organization’s values. 

Research shows when an employee’s values align with their organization’s values, they have the highest degree of job satisfaction. 

When there is a disconnect, it can lead to stress, disengagement, and turnover.

But that doesn’t mean the organization is “toxic.” It just means it’s the wrong fit.

The same environment can be very energizing to one person and soul crushing for another. 

Choose wisely.
firstComment: My resignation letter literally said my morals and ethics no longer aligned with theirs for my future goals. Walked away and it’s been the most important step in healing.
